I will talk about Bolinger Bands because I know you don't like them.  I remember my Economics 101 professor in college talking about equilibrium.  He siad that stock prices tend to remai in a steady state until something happens to change teh equilibrium point.  The Bollinger Bands.  Bollinger Bands were created by John Bollinger, in the early 1980s.  John used to be a prominent technical analyst on financial news stations.  Bollinger bands are based on standard deviations.  Bollinger bands tend to become more narrow preceding a change in equilibrium price, signalling a bounce or drop in the equilibrium price range.  They may not tell you the directiion of the move, but watching them as they widen should give you a clue about where th prices are headed.  I use them along with MACD and VWAP, as well as monitoring the real time volume and price movements.
